name: Gateau St. Honore
recipeInstructions:
1. To plump the raisins, heat brandy and raisins over low heat. Set aside to soak.
2. Chop 12 ounces chocolate into small pieces. Melt with butter in the top of a double boiler over barely simmering water. When melted, remove immediately and whisk in egg yolks. Add the plumped raisins. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Fold into the chocolate-raisin mixture. Cover and chill for 3 to 4 hours or overnight.
3. Roll puff pastry into a 12-inch circle about 1/4-inch thick. Pierce with a fork. Let rest for 25 minutes. Place on a baking sheet and bake at 450°F until golden brown, about 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Caramelize sugar in a heavy skillet over low heat until golden brown. Do not overbrown. Remove immediately from the heat.
5. Using a fork, quickly dip one half of each cream puff into caramel and place on buttered parchment paper. When caramel has set, dip the uncoated half in caramel. Cool.
6. Place caramel-coated cream puffs around the puff pastry. Fill the center with the chilled chocolate filling.
7. Melt remaining 3 ounces chocolate. Whip cream until soft peaks form. Fold melted chocolate into half of the whipped cream. Set aside.
8. Spoon the remaining half of whipped cream into a pastry bag fitted with a large round tube. Pipe dome-shaped balls in rows over the chocolate filling, leaving spaces between rows. Repeat, using chocolate-whipped cream, filling in the spaces between rows of white whipped cream to create stripes.
Makes one 12-inch cake
